title: HireIQ Frontend - AI-Powered Recruitment Management Platform

requirements.summary: Build sidebar navigation layout with header showing app logo, user role, and notification icon
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar navigation renders all listed sections
Active section is visually highlighted
Header shows app logo, user role, and notification icon
Layout is responsive and collapses sidebar on smaller screens

file_operations.description: Create main layout component with sidebar navigation (Dashboard, Jobs, Candidates, Pipeline, Interviews, Analytics, Templates, Offers, Settings) and top header displaying the generated logo asset frontend/public/assets/generated/hireiq-logo.dim_200x60.png, user role from authorization component, and notification bell icon. Implement responsive behavior that collapses sidebar on smaller screens.

Wrap the application router with the Layout component to apply sidebar and header globally. Use the authorization component to ensure users must be logged in before seeing application data. When logging out, clear all cached application data including user profile.
Update the favicon link to use the generated icon asset frontend/public/assets/generated/hireiq-icon.dim_64x64.png.

requirements.summary: Build Dashboard page displaying recruiter analytics with metrics, bar chart, and top AI-matched candidates
acceptanceCriteria: Metric cards display real data fetched from backend
Bar chart renders candidate counts per pipeline stage
Top 5 AI-matched candidates are listed with scores and job names

file_operations.description: Create Dashboard page displaying the generated banner asset frontend/public/assets/generated/dashboard-banner.dim_1200x300.png as a decorative header. Show metric cards for total open jobs, total active candidates, total interviews this week, and average time-to-hire fetched via getAnalytics backend capability. Render a bar chart of candidates per pipeline stage using the analytics data. Display a ranked list of top 5 AI-matched candidates across all open jobs with scores and job titles.

Create React Query hook to fetch analytics data from backend getAnalytics capability. Return analytics including totalOpenJobs, candidatesPerStage, averageTimeToHireDays, and topScoringCandidates with proper typing from backend interface.

requirements.summary: Build Jobs management page with searchable table, slide-over form, and View Candidates navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Jobs table displays all requisitions with search and filter by status/department
Create/edit form covers all job fields and submits successfully
Status badge reflects current job status
View Candidates navigates to the pipeline filtered by that job

file_operations.description: Create Jobs page with searchable and filterable table of job requisitions displaying title, department, location, job type, status badges, and actions. Fetch jobs via listJobs backend capability. Include search by title and filters for status and department. Provide 'View Candidates' action that navigates to Pipeline page with job filter applied. Wire route in App.tsx router.

Create slide-over form component for creating or editing jobs with fields: title, department, location, jobType (dropdown), description (textarea), requiredSkills (tag input), experienceLevel (number), and status (dropdown for edit mode). Submit via createJob or updateJob backend capabilities. Validate all required fields before submission.

Create React Query hooks for job operations: useListJobs (query listJobs), useCreateJob (mutation createJob), useUpdateJob (mutation updateJob), useUpdateJobStatus (mutation updateJobStatus), and useDeleteJob (mutation deleteJob, admin-only). Handle authorization errors gracefully with user-friendly messages.

requirements.summary: Build Candidates management page with searchable table, detail panel, and AI resume parsing
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate table is searchable and shows AI score and stage
Detail panel shows all profile fields, skill tags, and stage history
Resume paste area triggers backend parsing and populates the form
AI match score is visually displayed with a progress bar or gauge

file_operations.description: Create Candidates page with searchable table of candidates displaying name, email, phone, pipeline stage badge, applied jobs, and AI match score with visual progress indicator. Fetch candidates via listCandidates backend capability. Include search by name/email and filters by status and job. Wire route in App.tsx router.

Create candidate detail panel component showing full profile (name, email, phone, resume text, skills as tags, experience years), match score breakdown with visual gauge/progress bar, timeline of pipeline stage transitions with timestamps and notes, and list of linked interviews. Fetch candidate detail via getCandidate and interviews via getInterviewsByCandidate backend capabilities.

Create candidate form component with fields: name, email, phone, resume (textarea with paste area), skills (tag input), experienceYears (number). Include a 'Parse Resume' button that calls parseResume backend capability on the resume text and auto-fills extracted skills, experienceYears, education, and previousRoles into form fields. Submit via createCandidate or updateCandidate backend capabilities.

Create React Query hooks for candidate operations: useListCandidates (query listCandidates), useGetCandidate (query getCandidate by id), useCreateCandidate (mutation createCandidate), useUpdateCandidate (mutation updateCandidate), useDeleteCandidate (mutation deleteCandidate, admin-only), useParseResume (mutation parseResume), and useCalculateMatchScore (mutation calculateMatchScore). Handle authorization errors with user-friendly messages.

requirements.summary: Build Kanban-style Pipeline board with drag-and-drop stage transitions and job filtering
acceptanceCriteria: Kanban columns render for all pipeline stages
Candidate cards show name, job title, and AI match score
Dragging a card to a new column updates stage via backend call
Board can be filtered to show only candidates for a selected job

file_operations.description: Create Pipeline page with Kanban board displaying columns for each ATS stage: New, Screening, Shortlisted, Interview Scheduled, Offer Extended, Hired, Rejected. Show candidate cards with name, job title, and AI match score. Implement drag-and-drop using a library (e.g., react-beautiful-dnd or dnd-kit) to move candidates between columns, triggering moveCandidateStage backend capability with optional note dialog. Include job filter dropdown to show candidates for a selected job. Fetch candidates via listCandidates or getCandidatesByJob backend capabilities. Wire route in App.tsx router.
Create draggable candidate card component for the Kanban board showing candidate name, applied job title, and AI match score with visual indicator. Make card clickable to open CandidateDetail panel.

Create React Query hooks for pipeline operations: useGetCandidatesByStage (query getCandidatesByStage for a job and stage), useMoveCandidateStage (mutation moveCandidateStage with candidateId, newStatus, and optional note). Invalidate candidate queries on successful stage move.

requirements.summary: Build Interviews page with list/calendar toggle, schedule form, and status updates
acceptanceCriteria: Interviews are displayed in both list and simple calendar view
Schedule interview form links to candidate and job
Status can be updated to completed or cancelled
Upcoming interviews are highlighted

file_operations.description: Create Interviews page with toggle between list view and simple calendar view. List view displays scheduled interviews with candidate name, job title, interview type, date/time, interviewer, and status badge. Highlight upcoming interviews. Provide actions to schedule new interview, edit existing interview, and update status to completed or cancelled. Fetch all interviews by iterating candidates and calling getInterviewsByCandidate, or maintain a flat list via listCandidates and aggregate interviews. Wire route in App.tsx router.

Create interview form component with fields: candidateId (dropdown of candidates), jobId (dropdown of jobs), dateTime (datetime picker), interviewer (text), interviewType (dropdown: phone/video/onSite), status (dropdown: scheduled/completed/cancelled for edit mode), and notes (textarea). Submit via createInterview or updateInterview backend capabilities.

Create React Query hooks for interview operations: useGetInterview (query getInterview by id), useGetInterviewsByCandidate (query getInterviewsByCandidate), useCreateInterview (mutation createInterview), useUpdateInterview (mutation updateInterview), and useDeleteInterview (mutation deleteInterview, admin-only). Handle authorization errors gracefully.

requirements.summary: Build AI Chatbot widget with FAQ responses, quick-reply suggestions, and job data
acceptanceCriteria: Floating chat button is accessible on all pages
Chatbot responds to at least 10 common recruitment FAQs
Quick-reply suggestions are shown for common intents
Chatbot can list open job titles fetched from the backend

file_operations.description: Create floating chatbot widget component visible site-wide as a button in the bottom-right corner. Implement a simple intent-matching chatbot that responds to at least 10 common recruitment FAQs (e.g., 'How do I apply?', 'What jobs are open?', 'How long does hiring take?', 'What is the interview process?', 'Can I edit my application?', 'How do I check application status?', 'What documents do I need?', 'Is remote work available?', 'What benefits do you offer?', 'How do I contact HR?'). Show quick-reply suggestion buttons for common intents. For 'What jobs are open?', fetch and display open job titles via listJobs backend capability filtered by status open. Keep responses concise and intent-focused.
Include the ChatbotWidget component in the Layout so it appears on all pages as a floating element.

requirements.summary: Build Analytics page with line chart, donut chart, bar chart, and time-to-hire table
acceptanceCriteria: All four charts render with real backend data
Charts are labelled and include legends
Data auto-refreshes on page visit via React Query

file_operations.description: Create Analytics page with four visual charts: line chart for applications over time (last 30 days, derive from candidate createdAt timestamps), donut chart for pipeline stage distribution (use candidatesPerStage from analytics), bar chart for top jobs by application volume (count appliedJobIds per job), and a table of average time-to-hire per job (compute from candidate createdAt to hired status timestamp). Use a charting library (e.g., recharts or chart.js). Fetch data via getAnalytics and listCandidates backend capabilities. Use React Query to auto-refresh data on page visit. Wire route in App.tsx router.

requirements.summary: Build Templates management page with category filter, rich text editor, and live preview
acceptanceCriteria: Templates are listed and filterable by category
Create/edit form supports placeholder token insertion
Live preview renders template with sample candidate and job data

file_operations.description: Create Templates page listing all communication templates with name, subject, category, and actions. Include filter dropdown by category. Fetch templates via listTemplates backend capability. Provide actions to create, edit, and delete templates (admin-only for create/edit/delete). Wire route in App.tsx router.

Create template form component with fields: name, subject, body (textarea or simple rich text editor), category (dropdown or text). Include placeholder token insertion buttons for {{candidateName}}, {{jobTitle}}, {{interviewDate}}, {{companyName}}, etc. Show a live preview panel that renders the template with sample data substituted for placeholders. Submit via createTemplate or updateTemplate backend capabilities (admin-only).

Create React Query hooks for template operations: useListTemplates (query listTemplates), useGetTemplate (query getTemplate by id), useCreateTemplate (mutation createTemplate, admin-only), useUpdateTemplate (mutation updateTemplate, admin-only), and useDeleteTemplate (mutation deleteTemplate, admin-only). Handle authorization errors gracefully.

requirements.summary: Build Offers page with list, generation form, formatted preview, and status updates
acceptanceCriteria: Offers list shows all fields and status badges
Offer generation form populates all required fields
Formatted offer letter preview renders before saving
Status update (accept/decline) persists via backend call

file_operations.description: Create Offers page listing all generated offer letters with candidate name, job title, salary, start date, and status badge (pending/accepted/declined). Fetch offers by iterating candidates and calling getOffersByCandidate, or aggregate from listCandidates. Provide actions to generate new offer, view formatted preview, and update status. Wire route in App.tsx router.

Create offer generation form component with fields: candidateId (dropdown), jobId (dropdown), salary (number), startDate (date picker), clauses (textarea for custom clauses). Submit via createOfferLetter backend capability. After generation, display formatted offer letter preview using the returned letterText. Provide actions to update offer status to accepted or declined via updateOfferStatus backend capability.

Create React Query hooks for offer operations: useGetOfferLetter (query getOfferLetter by id), useGetOffersByCandidate (query getOffersByCandidate), useCreateOfferLetter (mutation createOfferLetter), useUpdateOfferStatus (mutation updateOfferStatus), and useDeleteOfferLetter (mutation deleteOfferLetter, admin-only). Handle authorization errors gracefully.

requirements.summary: Apply dark-mode-first design theme with teal and amber accents, sharp borders, and gradient highlights on AI elements
acceptanceCriteria: Dark background is applied globally
Teal and amber accents are used consistently for CTAs and highlights
AI score elements use gradient treatment to differentiate them visually
Typography and spacing are consistent across all pages

file_operations.description: Apply a bold, modern dark-mode-first design theme globally: use deep charcoal/slate background (#1a1a1a or similar), vibrant teal (#14b8a6 or similar) and amber (#f59e0b or similar) as accent colors for CTAs and highlights, sharp card borders with subtle shadows, clean sans-serif typography (e.g., Inter or system fonts), and consistent spacing. Define CSS custom properties for theme colors and reuse them throughout.

Extend Tailwind config to include custom dark theme colors: primary teal, secondary amber, dark backgrounds (charcoal/slate shades), and border colors. Define gradient utilities for AI score highlights (e.g., teal-to-amber gradient). Ensure all components use these theme tokens consistently.

Create reusable AI score badge component displaying a candidate's match score with a gradient background (teal-to-amber) and a progress bar or circular gauge. Use this component consistently across Candidates, Pipeline, and Dashboard to visually differentiate AI-scored elements.

requirements.summary: Wire generated hero logo asset in sidebar and favicon, and dashboard banner in Dashboard page
acceptanceCriteria: Logo appears in the sidebar at correct dimensions
Dashboard banner is rendered at the top of the Dashboard page
Assets are loaded from frontend/public/assets/generated

file_operations.description: Update Layout component to display the generated logo asset frontend/public/assets/generated/hireiq-logo.dim_200x60.png in the sidebar header at 200x60 dimensions. Ensure the logo is visible and properly styled within the dark theme.

Update Dashboard page to render the generated banner asset frontend/public/assets/generated/dashboard-banner.dim_1200x300.png as a decorative header at the top of the page at 1200x300 dimensions. Ensure the banner integrates seamlessly with the page layout and dark theme.
Update the favicon link to reference the generated icon asset frontend/public/assets/generated/hireiq-icon.dim_64x64.png at 64x64 dimensions for the browser tab icon.
